---
title: "FVW"
author: "Liz Chen"
date: "2026-02-10"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggExtra)
library(cowplot)
library(ACER)
library(CMplot)
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)

setwd("~/Documents/SSD_Data")
```

```{r}
FVW <- read.csv("Cleaned up FVW.csv")
head(FVW)

#Clean up sex factor
FVW$sex <- as.factor(FVW$sex)
levels(FVW$sex) <- c("NA", "NA", "NA", "NA", "F", "F", "M", "M", "NA")
FVW$sex <- factor(FVW$sex)  # Remove NA levels

#Filter for top 10% (use 0.9 for top 10%)
FVWtop10 <- FVW %>%
  group_by(lineage, sex) %>%
  filter(lnpupa >= quantile(lnpupa, probs = 0.9, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

#Count how many females and males per lineage
lineage_counts <- FVWtop10 %>%
  group_by(lineage, sex) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = count, values_fill = 0)

#Filter lineages that have at least 5 females and 5 males
valid_lineages <- lineage_counts %>%
  filter(F >= 5 & M >= 5) %>%
  pull(lineage)

FVW_filtered <- FVWtop10 %>%
  filter(lineage %in% valid_lineages)

#Calculate means
means10 <- FVW_filtered %>%
  group_by(lineage, sex) %>%
  summarise(mean_pupa = mean(lnpupa, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

#Calculate SSD
FVWSSD10 <- means10 %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = mean_pupa) %>%
  mutate(SSD = F - M)

#Plot
ggplot(FVWSSD10, aes(x = SSD)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20) + 
  theme_cowplot() +
  labs(title = "Sexual Size Dimorphism (Top 10% by Lineage)",
       x = "SSD (Female - Male)",
       y = "Count")
```

rank plot
```{r}
ggplot(FVWSSD10, aes(x = reorder(lineage, -SSD), y = SSD)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = reorder(lineage, -SSD),
                   xend = reorder(lineage, -SSD),
                   y = 0, yend = SSD),
               color = "gray", lwd = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3, pch = 21, bg = 4, col = 1) +
  xlab("Group") +
  ylab("") +
  theme_cowplot()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                   vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1))
ggsave("~/Documents/SSD_Data/Thesis/figures/FVW_rank.svg", width = 6, height = 6, bg = "white")
```

morphospace
```{r}
head(FVWSSD10)
p <- ggplot(FVWSSD10, aes(x = M, y = F)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "grey") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits=c(14.7, (14.7+0.25)), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(14.57, (14.57+.25)), expand = c(0, 0))+
  theme_cowplot()

pDense <- ggMarginal(p, fill="#2297e6")
pDense
ggsave("~/Documents/SSD_Data/Thesis/figures/FVW_FvM.svg", width = 6, height = 6, bg = "white")
```

SSD for all lines with more than five females and five males
```{r}
#Count how many females and males per lineage
all_lineage_counts <- FVW %>%
  group_by(lineage, sex) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = count, values_fill = 0)

#Filter lineages that have at least 5 females and 5 males
all_valid_lineages <- all_lineage_counts %>%
  filter(F >= 5 & M >= 5) %>%
  pull(lineage)

all_FVW_filtered <- FVW %>%
  filter(lineage %in% all_valid_lineages)

#Calculate means
means <- all_FVW_filtered %>%
  group_by(lineage, sex) %>%
  summarise(mean_pupa = mean(lnpupa, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

#Calculate SSD
FVWSSD <- means %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = mean_pupa) %>%
  mutate(SSD = F - M)

#Plot
ggplot(FVWSSD, aes(x = SSD)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20) + 
  theme_cowplot() +
  labs(title = "Sexual Size Dimorphism Unfiltered",
       x = "SSD (Female - Male)",
       y = "Count")
```
